speaker: Mike
content: Bongo! Get down! Down！I'm sorry. So who's the guy that lives across from Mrs. Huber again?
mark: live across from sb 住在某人的对面 live across the street 住在街对面

speaker: Susan
content: That's Mr. Mullen. And just so you know, if he invites you in, you have to meet all of his pets.
mark: invite sb in 邀请某人进来

speaker: Susan
mark: taxidermist n. 动物标本剥制者

speaker: Mike
content: Oh, OK, thanks for the warning.
mark: thanks for the warning. 谢谢你的提醒。

speaker: Eddie
content: Isn't Mr. Mullen's brother your divorce attorney?
mark: attorney n. 律师；代理人 = lawyer

speaker: Eddie
content: Can I say something? In my heart, I still believe that you and Carl are gonna get back together.
mark: get back together 复合

speaker: Eddie
content: Oh, yeah. I've never seen two people more in love. I mean, she's never gonna find that kind of chemistry with another man, never.
mark: I've never seen two people more in love 我从未见过比他们更相爱的两个人
be never going to do sth 永远不，口语化，书面语中通常会用 will never do sth
chemistry n. 化学；化学成分，化学反应

speaker: Alice
content: It was everything Susan could do to keep a smile on her face, round two was under way, and she was already taking a beating.
mark: It was everything sb could do to do sth 这个句型强调某人做某事极为艰难，需要使出浑身解数才能做到
under way 进行中；已开始并进行着 underway 可以使用但不够规范
take a beating 遭受重创; 遭受挫折或损失

speaker: Alice
content: What Eddie hadn’t counted on was that Susan had someone else in her corner.
mark: What Eddie hadn't counted on was that Susan had someone else in her corner. 伊迪没有料到的是苏珊有人支持她。
表语从句：was 后面的整个句子作表语。引导表语从句的 that 一律不能省略。
count on sth 指望；依靠；依赖（某事物）
sb hadn't counted on sth 某人没有料到某事；某人未曾想到某事会发生
in sb's corner 支持某人；站在某人一边

speaker: Julie
content: You know what, Mrs. Britt, Do you know who I always liked? Mr. Rothwell, your fourth husband.

speaker: Eddie
content: He was my second husband, I've only been married twice.
mark: be married to sb 与某人结婚，强调的是一种婚姻状态, be married 是一个表示婚姻状态的短语
marry sb 与某人结婚，强调的是结婚这个动作,不能用 marry to sb 来表达

speaker: Julie
content: Twice? You weren't married to the guy with all the tattoos that they took away in handcuffs?
mark: 发音：weren't：əː → 饿 、won't：oʊ → 欧 、want：ɑː → 啊
tattoo n. 刺青，纹身
they took away in handcuffs "they" 是一个泛指，用来指代那些负责执法和维护治安的人。 take away 带走
handcuff n. 手铐 in handcuffs 戴着手铐

speaker: Susan
content: No, Julie, she wasn't married to Javier, that was just one of her special friends.

speaker: Susan
content: I think we should change the subject. You know, unless you want to keep talking about it.
mark: change the subject 转换话题
